steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: a = advanced skaters in 2010, b = intermediate skaters in 2010. Each group grows by its own rate; the 2020 totals must still sum correctly. Advanced grew 53% → multiply by 1.53. Intermediate grew 44% → multiply by 1.44. The overall total of 35 grew 49% → multiply 35 by 1.49. The equation sets the two 2020 group counts equal to the 2020 total.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$1.53a + 1.49b = 35(1.44)$': uses 1.49 (the total's growth rate) for intermediate instead of 1.44, and uses 1.44 (intermediate's rate) on the right side for the total. Wrong rates on wrong groups. ✗
  • Choice B — '$1.49a + 0.53b = 35(1.44)$': applies the total's multiplier to advanced and treats 53% increase as 0.53 (a decrease). Right side also misuses 1.44. ✗
  • Choice C — '$1.53a + 1.44b = 35(1.49)$': advanced × 1.53, intermediate × 1.44, total = 35 × 1.49. Each multiplier goes to the correct group. ✓
  • Choice D — '$1.44a + 1.53b = 35(1.49)$': swaps the multipliers — gives advanced the intermediate rate and vice versa. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
